concept: Light (Weight) / Not Heavy / Easy to Lift
description: Of little weight; not heavy or burdensome. 'Phelo' names the quality of lightness—physical ease of carrying, metaphorical freedom from weight.
sound_symbolism: The word lifts easily. 'Phe' /ɸe/ begins with airy breath, weight released. Then 'lo' floats with ease, lightness embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phelo tana' (light burden). Opposite of 'tumoa' (heavy). Distinct from brightness—use 'keru' (bright) for light as illumination.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Lightness comes from releasing attachment—the unburdened mind moves freely.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Light tools enabled sustained work—weight was managed through design and technique.
